text: **Speaker 1:** Hello, I'm Dr.
**Speaker 1:** Jill.
**Speaker 1:** Welcome to Let's Learn English with Anna.
**Speaker 1:** A {hobby|趣味|/ˈhɒbi/|My hobby is reading books.} can be any activity.
**Speaker 1:** You do them in your {free time|自由時間|/friː taɪm/|I play games in my free time.}

**Speaker 1:** {Drawing|描くこと/絵を描くこと|/ˈdrɔːɪŋ/|Drawing is a relaxing hobby.}, {painting|絵を描くこと/塗装|/ˈpeɪntɪŋ/|Painting helps me express myself.}, and {gardening|園芸|/ˈɡɑːrdənɪŋ/|Gardening is my weekend hobby.} are popular hobbies.
**Speaker 1:** Some people make things or {collect|集める|/kəˈlekt/|I collect stamps as a hobby.} things as a hobby.
**Speaker 1:** What is your hobby?
**Speaker 1:** That is a question Anna helps us answer.
**Speaker 1:** What is your hobby?
Oh hi!
I want to show you something.
This
Is a {metal detector|金属探知機|/ˈmetl dɪˈtektər/|A metal detector finds hidden objects.}
It {detects|検出する/見つける|/dɪˈtekts/|Sensors detect movement.} or finds metal.
I looked for hours and found this
You found one big old {nail|釘|/neɪl/|He hammered a nail into the wood.}
It is a metal {spike|尖った金属片/釘|/spaɪk/|A metal spike can be sharp.} from an old railroad.
You need to find something else.
What Daisy?
A new hobby!
Very funny.
But I am looking for a new hobby.
**Speaker 3:** I tried {bird watching|野鳥観察|/bɜːrd ˈwɑːtʃɪŋ/|Bird watching is peaceful.}
I tried {rock climbing|ロッククライミング|/rɒk ˈklaɪmɪŋ/|Rock climbing is an exciting sport.}
Are those your hobbies now?
Um, no.
There is a {kite|凧|/kaɪt/|Let's fly a kite today.} festival in DC.
Flying kites!
Great idea, Max!
Or you can keep {looking|探す/見つける|/ˈlʊkɪŋ/|Keep looking for the answer.} for more {big|大きな|/bɪɡ/|A big dog is barking.} old {nails|釘|/neɪlz/|Hammer the nails into the wood.} in the dirt

**Speaker 1:** {Birdwatching|野鳥観察|/ˈbɜːrdˌwɑːtʃɪŋ/|Birdwatching is a relaxing hobby.}, rock {climbing|登山/クライミング|/ˈklaɪmɪŋ/|Rock climbing is exciting.}, kite {flying|凧揚げ|/ˈflaɪɪŋ/|Kite flying is fun in the park.}.
**Speaker 1:** What do these {hobbies|趣味|/ˈhɑːbiz/|I have many hobbies.} have in common?
**Speaker 1:** They all use a special type of English word.
**Speaker 1:** You start with a {verb|動詞|/vɜːrb/|A verb shows action.}, then you add ing to the end, and you get a special type of noun.
**Speaker 1:** They help us talk about actions in a new way.
**Speaker 1:** Let's see some examples.
**Speaker 1:** My hobby is {playing|遊ぶ/演奏する|/ˈpleɪɪŋ/|I enjoy playing games.} Roblox.
Bake I {bake|焼く|/beɪk/|I bake cookies.} cakes.
Baking is a popular hobby.
What is a popular hobby?
Baking.
Sing.
I {sing|歌う|/sɪŋ/|I sing in the choir.} every day.
I like {singing|歌うこと|/ˈsɪŋɪŋ/|Singing makes me happy.}
What does she like?
Singing build
I {build|建てる/作る|/bɪld/|I build model cars.} things.
I like {building|建設/製作|/ˈbɪldɪŋ/|Building things is fun.}
**Speaker 1:** Great job.
**Speaker 1:** Next we hear from some friends.
**Speaker 1:** They talk about their hobbies.
I have lots of hobbies.
In my {spare|空いた/余分な|/sper/|I have spare time.} time I like to read and paint.
**Speaker 7:** In my {spare|空いた/余分な|/sper/|I have spare time.} time I like {stargazing|星空観察|/ˈstɑːrˌɡeɪzɪŋ/|Stargazing is peaceful.}
**Speaker 8:** My hobby is {drove|運転した|/droʊv/|I drove to the store.}
**Speaker 8:** In my spare time, I like {blank|空白の/無地の|/blæŋk/|A blank page is empty.} chairs.
**Speaker 8:** In my spare time, I like {making|作ること|/ˈmeɪkɪŋ/|Making things is creative.} Legos
**Speaker 1:** Thanks friends!
**Speaker 1:** Now let's go back to Anna.
**Speaker 1:** She wants to try her {hobby|趣味|/ˈhɒbi/|My hobby is reading books.}
**Speaker 1:** So she is at an outdoor {kite|凧|/kaɪt/|Let's fly a kite together.} party.
**Speaker 1:** Well, of course it is {outdoors|屋外で|/ˌaʊtˈdɔːrz/|We play outdoors in summer.}
**Speaker 1:** Let's watch.
I am at an outdoor kite party.
Yeah!
I see so many {kites|凧|/kaɪts/|Colorful kites are flying high.}
I love kites.
You are on the ground, but the kite is in the {air|空中|/ɛr/|Birds fly in the air.}
But I cannot {fly|飛ばす|/flaɪ/|Let's fly a kite together.} a kite.
It never leaves the ground.
Maybe today I can learn to fly a kite.
I did it!
I flew a kite!
Young people, old people, everyone can fly a kite.
**Speaker 1:** What is your hobby?
**Speaker 1:** Do you have more than one?
**Speaker 1:** Now you can talk about your hobbies.
**Speaker 1:** See you next time on Let's Learn English with Anna.
